title: Delete File
content:
In this example we will delete a file stored on the Kelvin Platform and receive the confirmation. You can only delete one file at a time. You need to use the `id` of the file which will be a UUID number. You can not use the filename. The delete is permanent and not recoverable.
=== "Kelvin API" ```bash title="API cURL Example" linenums="1" curl -X "POST" \ "https:///api/v4/filestorage/66fce65f-d43f-4424-89b8-e1dd01f9193b/delete" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Accept: application/json" \ -d '' ``` The response code will be 200 or an error code from 4XX. === "Kelvin API Client (Python)" ```python title="API Client (Python) Example" linenums="1" from kelvin.api.client import Client # Login client = Client(url="https://", username="") client.login(password="") # Delete File in Filestorage response = client.filestorage.delete_file( file_id='0107549c-2069-4824-bc4f-30c7bc94172e' ) print(response) ``` You will see the output like this; ``` title="API Client (Python) Example Response" linenums="1" None ```

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api.client import Client

    # Login
    client = Client(url="https://<url.kelvin.ai>", username="<your_username>")
    client.login(password="<your_password>")

    # Delete File in Filestorage
    response = client.filestorage.delete_file(
        file_id='0107549c-2069-4824-bc4f-30c7bc94172e'
    )

    print(response)
code_examples.title: API Client (Python) Example
